target.label: packages/devbox/ absorb from cc-devbox — image + compose + substrate tooling access (M0.5 a-c)

rationale: Infrastructure-smoke substrate story — first of its class; extends the documentation-surface + configuration-surface WAIVED precedent (Stories 1.7 iter-4 + 1.8 iter-3 + 1.9 iter-3 + 1.10 iter-46 + 1.11 iter-57 + 1.12 iter-64 + 1.13 iter-71 + 1.14 iter-78 + 1.15 iter-85 + 1.16 iter-92) to the Docker/docker-compose/shell-script runtime-infrastructure class. Story v1.2 iter-98 Change Log row (ATDD-skip) explicitly defers per-AC automated coverage to Story 2.1-test-runner-landing (Epic 13 scope) + operator-workstation follow-up runs (AC 3 compose run + AC 4 benchmark) via hybrid ground-(c) variant-(ii)+(iii) ATDD-skip clause, with ground-(d) upstream-provenance-precedent added for the infrastructure-smoke class (upstream cc-devbox itself has no test suite — inspection of github.com/tthew/cc-devbox shows Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml + entrypoint.sh + whitelist.sh + bash scripts with no red-phase harness; absorbing cc-devbox into packages/devbox/ preserves that posture faithfully; substituting a Playwright/Vitest scaffold at absorb time would introduce a testing class Ralph has not yet decided to adopt for runtime-infrastructure artefacts — deferred to Epic 13's framework landing + per-package policy). (a) substrate-verification-covers-ACs: Task 7 four-check structural bundle — (1) entrypoint.sh forbidden-pattern grep → exit 1 (no `npm install`, `pip install`, `curl|sh`, `wget|sh` patterns); (2) Dockerfile head -5 → pin-rationale comment block as first content BEFORE `FROM ubuntu:24.04` directive; (3) `docker compose -f packages/devbox/docker-compose.yml config` → exit 0 with resolved YAML valid; (4) `git ls-files -s packages/devbox/entrypoint.sh packages/devbox/scripts/benchmark.sh` → `100755` for both. Task 4.1 image-bake (iter-123): keel-devbox:local sha256:e7e91f1537f196b39f32ae6168d33bb33fd58b76cc7a9a53582800ff4038f7a2 (847,527,165 bytes ≈ 848 MB, linux/arm64) from `docker compose build` exit 0 on backend B safe-subset path. All four structural smokes + the bake persistence re-verified LIVE at iter-126 trace-time — byte-identical to iter-123/iter-124 dev-story + iter-125 codification records. (b) no test runner at Story 2.1 time — Epic 13 scope; recursive probe for vitest/jest/playwright/cypress configs returns zero matches at iter-126. (c) HYBRID variant-(ii)+(iii)+(d)-upstream-provenance: variant (ii) downstream-operator-workstation-covers-integration — Tasks 4.2 + 4.3 (compose run pnpm test/lint exit 0) + Task 5.2 (benchmark.sh cold + warm run appending to README § Benchmarks) are operator-owned carve-outs resolved by either (A) M4-Pro native with worktree under host-shared `/Users/...` (backend-B File Sharing allowlist overlap), or (B) backend-A isolated DinD (iteration container owns its own daemon — no host-allowlist dependency). Iter-124 proved the carve-out is NOT a compose/benchmark.sh defect: `docker compose config` PASS + `benchmark.sh --skip-cold` exits cleanly via `set -euo pipefail` with no corrupt README entry. The carve-out is scope-clarification-consistent — AC 4's original scope already placed NFR2 measurement on M4-Pro operator workstation; iter-124 extends that same operator-owned envelope to AC 3 dynamic verification under the specific backend-B + iteration-container topology. Variant (iii) spec-declared-CR-substitution — § Testing Standards subsection affirmatively delegates AC 1 + AC 2 + AC 3 + AC 4 adversarial coverage to iter-127 `/bmad-code-review (args: "2")` Blind Hunter / Edge Case Hunter / Acceptance Auditor fan-out (Dockerfile pin-rationale factual accuracy + apt-list completeness + Playwright deps diff + entrypoint.sh grep regex false-negatives + benchmark.sh shell-hygiene + README measurement-method verbatim consistency with AC 4). Ground-(d) upstream-provenance-precedent: upstream cc-devbox has no test suite — the absorb faithfully preserves that posture; this is the FIRST infrastructure-smoke class WAIVED precedent (Epic 1's ten were documentation-surface + configuration-surface classes). The deterministic FAIL signal (overall 0% < 80%) is a structural false-positive; no test runner is wired at Story 2.1 substrate stage. **ELEVENTH CUMULATIVE WAIVED PRECEDENT** (Stories 1.7 → 1.16 = 10; Story 2.1 = 11) — extends the established pattern to the first infrastructure-smoke class story. Structural trace-artefact inheritance from Stories 1.11-1.16 (~90% reasoning-cost savings per iter-46/57/64/71/78/85/92 precedent; mechanical adaptation of 1-16 template to 2.1's 4-AC infrastructure-smoke substrate with Docker/compose/shell-specific evidence replacing documentation/config evidence). Story 2.1 ships a NEW SURFACE CLASS vs Story 1.16 (runtime-infrastructure: Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml + entrypoint.sh + benchmark.sh + VERSIONS.md + expanded README.md vs 1.16's scaffold-template + rationale-doc + knowledge-file edits), but the substrate-authoring + operator-owned-carve-out + WAIVED-rationale posture is structurally identical. Backend-B safety-gate reference implementation (benchmark.sh detect_backend() + destructive-op refusal per INV-devbox-dind-available § Safety rule) landed iter-122 — first-in-class safety invariant enforcement for infrastructure-smoke stories. Story 2.1 advances Story State `in-dev → traced` (FR14n lifecycle matrix); next QUEUE item: `/bmad-create-story (args: "review")` post-dev SM requirements-satisfaction verification (`traced → sm-verified` or `sm-fixes-pending`; SM gate decides acceptance of the operator-owned carve-out per iter-125 Status-flip to `review`).
